rationale: In the theory sections, the cited paper is invoked more than once as part of the core justification and self-control literature, not as a peripheral mention. The authors explicitly contrast prior work on justifying indulgence with their own claim that justifications can also facilitate restraint, making the citation part of their conceptual positioning and contribution. Although cited alongside other papers, the functional role is clear and tied to hypothesis framing rather than a generic literature list.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as prior evidence that justifications can license indulgent, self-control-relaxing choices. It then extends that line by arguing and showing that token justifications can also resolve conflict in the opposite direction, increasing farsighted or normative choices in intertemporal and risky decisions.
